About the ACU Global Grants 2026

The Association of Commonwealth Universities (ACU) is one of the world’s oldest international university networks, bringing together higher education institutions across Commonwealth nations to promote collaboration, innovation, and sustainable development.

Through the ACU Global Grants 2026, undergraduate students are given the opportunity to undertake short-term academic programmes abroad within the Commonwealth. These programmes may include summer schools or winter schools that complement students’ academic studies while exposing them to new learning environments and international perspectives.

The grants aim to:

  • Support academic excellence.
  • Encourage international mobility among undergraduate students.
  • Promote knowledge exchange across Commonwealth universities.
  • Foster collaboration between institutions.
  • Enhance students’ global outlook and intercultural understanding.

A number of these grants are generously funded through the legacy of the HW Paxton Trust, demonstrating a continued commitment to expanding educational opportunities for Commonwealth students.

What Does the ACU Global Grant Cover?

The ACU Global Grants 2026 provide financial support for undergraduate students participating in eligible short-term academic programmes.

Grant Value

The programme offers:

  • 10 Global Grants available in total.
  • Each award is worth up to GBP 5,000.

The funding is intended to help cover costs associated with attending an eligible short-term academic programme abroad.

Allocation of Awards

The available grants are divided into two categories:

  • Five awards are reserved for citizens of the United Kingdom, funded through the HW Paxton Trust.
  • Five awards are available for citizens of Commonwealth countries (excluding the United Kingdom) or individuals holding refugee status within a Commonwealth country.

Benefits of the ACU Global Grants

Students selected for the ACU Global Grants will benefit from numerous academic and professional development opportunities, including:

  • Funding of up to GBP 5,000.
  • Participation in an international summer or winter school.
  • Exposure to different academic systems and teaching methods.
  • Opportunities to collaborate with students from across the Commonwealth.
  • Enhanced intercultural communication skills.
  • Broader international academic experience.
  • Stronger academic and professional networks.
  • Greater global awareness and cross-cultural understanding.

The programme supports not only financial access to international education but also the development of globally engaged graduates.

Who Can Apply?

The ACU Global Grants 2026 are open to undergraduate students studying at eligible universities within the Association of Commonwealth Universities network.

Applicants must satisfy all of the following eligibility requirements.

1. University Enrollment

Applicants must:

  • Be currently registered as an undergraduate student at an ACU member institution.

2. Citizenship or Refugee Status

Applicants must:

  • Be a citizen of a Commonwealth country, or
  • Hold refugee status in a Commonwealth country.

3. Eligible Academic Programme

Applicants must identify an academic programme related to their studies.

The proposed programme must:

  • Be a short-term academic programme, such as:
    • Summer school
    • Winter school
  • Be hosted by an ACU member institution.
  • Be located in a different Commonwealth country.
  • Have an official start date between:

1 December 2026 and 31 August 2027

4. Institutional Support

Applicants must provide:

  • A letter of institutional support from either:
    • Their departmental head, or
    • Their academic/course supervisor.
  • A letter of support from the proposed host institution.

Only applicants meeting all eligibility requirements will be considered.

Why Apply for the ACU Global Grants?

The ACU Global Grants offer undergraduate students an excellent opportunity to gain international academic experience while receiving financial assistance.

Participating in a short-term international academic programme allows students to:

  • Develop new academic knowledge.
  • Experience different teaching and learning environments.
  • Build international friendships and professional networks.
  • Enhance communication and intercultural competencies.
  • Improve future employability through international exposure.
  • Strengthen academic collaboration between Commonwealth universities.

For many students, the programme serves as a stepping stone toward future international study, research, or professional opportunities.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the application process through the official ACU platform.

Step 1

Review the:

  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
  • Official Application Guidance

Step 2

Submit an application using the official online application form.

Step 3

Applicants who already have a MyACU account can log in directly and access the application form.

Step 4

Applicants who do not yet have a MyACU account should:

  1. Register for a MyACU account.
  2. Verify their account through the registration email.
  3. Log into the platform.
  4. Complete and submit the application.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare all supporting documentation before beginning the online application.

APPLY HERE

Important Dates

Applications Open: 14 July 2026

Application Deadline: 25 August 2026 at 15:00 UTC

Late applications may not be accepted.

Conclusion

The ACU Global Grants 2026 provide an outstanding opportunity for undergraduate students across the Commonwealth to gain valuable international academic experience while receiving financial support of up to GBP 5,000. By enabling students to participate in short-term summer and winter schools hosted by ACU member universities, the programme promotes academic excellence, international collaboration, and cultural exchange across the Commonwealth.

Eligible undergraduate students are encouraged to begin preparing their supporting documents and submit their applications before the 25 August 2026 (15:00 UTC) deadline.

For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Discover more global job opportunities, click here.

The post ACU Global Grants 2026: Up to GBP 5,000 for Undergraduate Students to Attend International Summer and Winter Schools in Commonwealth Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid U.S. Helsinki Commission Fellowships for Graduate Students in International Affairs, Human Rights, and Strategic Communications
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 are now open for applications, offering an outstanding opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international affairs, human rights, security policy, communications, and government. Hosted by the Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe (CSCE), also known as the U.S. Helsinki Commission, these prestigious paid fellowships provide participants […]

The post Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid U.S. Helsinki Commission Fellowships for Graduate Students in International Affairs, Human Rights, and Strategic Communications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 are now open for applications, offering an outstanding opportunity for graduate students and recent graduates interested in international affairs, human rights, security policy, communications, and government. Hosted by the Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe (CSCE), also known as the U.S. Helsinki Commission, these prestigious paid fellowships provide participants with hands-on experience working alongside leading experts on some of the most pressing political, economic, military, and human rights issues affecting Europe, Eurasia, and North America.

Named in honor of Ambassador Max Kampelman, a distinguished American diplomat and long-time representative to the Conference on Security and Cooperation in Europe, the fellowship is designed to prepare the next generation of American leaders in foreign policy, strategic communications, democracy promotion, and international security.

Successful fellows will spend three months working in person in Washington, DC, contributing to research, policy development, communications, congressional activities, and public engagement while building valuable professional networks within the U.S. government.

Applications for the Fall 2026 cohort are currently open, with a submission deadline of 29 July 2026.

About the Max Kampelman Fellowships

The Max Kampelman Fellowship Program is administered by the Commission on Security and Cooperation in Europe (CSCE), commonly known as the U.S. Helsinki Commission.

The Commission works to strengthen:

  • Human rights
  • Democratic governance
  • Military security
  • Economic cooperation
  • Rule of law
  • Freedom of expression
  • International stability

Its work spans 57 participating States of the Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe (OSCE) across Europe, Eurasia, and North America.

The fellowship provides emerging professionals with the opportunity to gain practical experience inside a U.S. government commission that directly supports Congress on international policy issues.

Participants become part of a multidisciplinary team that contributes to hearings, legislative work, research publications, diplomatic engagement, and communications initiatives.

Fellowship Overview

Selected fellows will:

  • Work directly with Commission staff and policy experts
  • Participate in congressional and policy activities
  • Conduct research on international affairs
  • Attend hearings, conferences, and briefings
  • Contribute to official publications
  • Build professional networks
  • Gain practical experience in U.S. foreign policy and international governance

The fellowship combines academic learning with practical government experience, making it ideal for individuals pursuing careers in:

  • Government
  • International organizations
  • Human rights advocacy
  • Public policy
  • Diplomacy
  • Strategic communications
  • Journalism
  • International development

Fellowship Duration

The fellowship lasts:

  • Three months

Participants are expected to:

  • Work approximately 30 hours per week
  • Attend the fellowship in person
  • Be based at the Commission’s office in Washington, DC

Fellowship Benefits

Successful fellows will receive numerous professional and financial benefits, including:

Financial Support

Participants receive:

  • Hourly salary of USD $27 per hour

Professional Development

Fellows benefit from:

  • Professional mentoring
  • Career development sessions
  • Networking opportunities
  • Interaction with senior Commission staff
  • Exposure to congressional processes
  • Practical policy experience

Government Experience

Participants gain experience in:

  • Congressional operations
  • Legislative processes
  • International diplomacy
  • Public policy development
  • Government research
  • Strategic communications

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants may apply for one of two fellowship tracks.

1. Policy Fellowship

Policy Fellows work alongside Commission policy advisors on issues related to:

  • Political affairs
  • Military security
  • Human rights
  • Economic cooperation
  • Environmental issues
  • International relations

Responsibilities

Policy Fellows may:

  • Conduct policy research
  • Monitor developments across the OSCE region
  • Assist with congressional hearings
  • Support legislative activities
  • Prepare briefing materials
  • Assist with congressional delegations
  • Contribute to Commission publications
  • Attend hearings and policy events
  • Perform administrative support duties
  • Write at least one article for publication on the Commission website

2. Communications Fellowship

Communications Fellows support the Commission’s outreach and public engagement activities.

Responsibilities

These include:

  • Supporting media relations
  • Assisting with communications campaigns
  • Promoting Commission hearings
  • Publicizing events
  • Creating website content
  • Producing social media content
  • Supporting special communications initiatives
  • Staffing Commission events
  • Writing at least one article for publication
  • Performing communications-related administrative duties

Why Apply?

The fellowship provides a rare opportunity to work within a U.S. congressional commission focused on international affairs.

Benefits include:

  • Paid government fellowship
  • Practical foreign policy experience
  • Exposure to congressional work
  • Human rights policy experience
  • Security policy experience
  • Communications experience
  • Legislative research experience
  • Publication opportunities
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals
  • Career networking
  • Strong résumé enhancement

Areas of Work

Fellows may engage with topics such as:

  • International security
  • Democracy
  • Elections
  • Human rights
  • Military affairs
  • Economic cooperation
  • Environmental policy
  • Strategic communications
  • Foreign policy
  • International organizations
  • OSCE affairs
  • Congressional diplomacy

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must satisfy the eligibility criteria established by the Commission.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Current graduate students
  • Registered graduate students
  • Recent undergraduate graduates (provided the fellowship begins within one year of graduation)
  • Exceptional undergraduate students with previous internship experience

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in international affairs
  • Interest in Congress and U.S. government
  • Interest in foreign policy
  • Interest in international security
  • Interest in democracy and human rights

Knowledge of another OSCE language is considered an advantage.

Citizenship Requirements

Applicants must meet one of the following requirements:

  • Be a United States citizen
  • Be a lawful permanent resident pursuing U.S. citizenship
  • Be a refugee or asylum recipient meeting statutory requirements
  • Owe allegiance to the United States

These requirements are established under applicable U.S. federal legislation governing employment eligibility.

Qualifications for Policy Fellowship

Competitive applicants should possess:

  • Excellent writing skills
  • Strong analytical abilities
  • Research experience
  • Oral communication skills
  • Interest in government
  • Interest in international relations
  • Interest in human rights

Qualifications for Communications Fellowship

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Excellent writing skills
  • Editing skills
  • Basic knowledge of WordPress
  • Familiarity with social media platforms
  • Interest in government communications

Additional assets include:

  • Photography
  • Graphic design
  • Video production
  • Creative content development

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  1. Brief cover letter

The letter should explain:

  • Why you wish to work for the Commission
  • Relevant educational or personal background
  • Specific policy interests
  • Availability, including preferred start and end dates and weekly availability
  1. Resume
  • Maximum two pages
  1. Writing sample
  • Maximum three pages
  1. Optional portfolio (Communications Fellowship only)

May include:

  • Graphic design samples
  • Copywriting
  • Photography
  • Videos
  • Digital communications work

Application Instructions

Applications should be submitted by email.

Applicants must:

  • Email all required materials
  • Use the correct subject line
  • Indicate whether applying for:
    • Policy Fellowship
    • Communications Fellowship

Only complete applications submitted before the deadline will be considered.

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews.

Applicants are requested not to contact the Commission regarding application status.

Upcoming Fellowship Term

Policy Fellowship

  • Fall 2026
  • Applications currently open

Communications Fellowship

  • Fall 2026
  • Applications currently open

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications for both fellowship tracks is:

29 July 2026

Applications received after the deadline or missing required documents will not be considered.

Career Opportunities After the Fellowship

The Max Kampelman Fellowship offers excellent preparation for careers in:

  • U.S. Government
  • Congress
  • International organizations
  • Foreign service
  • Human rights organizations
  • International NGOs
  • Public policy institutes
  • Think tanks
  • Strategic communications
  • Journalism
  • International development
  • Academia

Participants gain practical skills and professional networks that can significantly strengthen future career prospects.

Tips for Preparing a Competitive Application

To improve your chances of selection:

  • Tailor your cover letter to the Commission’s mission.
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in international affairs, democracy, and human rights.
  • Showcase analytical, research, or communications experience.
  • Submit a polished writing sample that reflects your strongest work.
  • Communications applicants should include a high-quality portfolio if available.
  • Highlight language skills, international experience, leadership roles, or previous internships relevant to the fellowship.

Conclusion

The Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 provide an exceptional opportunity for graduate students, recent graduates, and aspiring professionals interested in international affairs, human rights, security policy, and strategic communications. Through paid, hands-on experience at the U.S. Helsinki Commission in Washington, DC, fellows gain invaluable exposure to congressional processes, foreign policy, and international cooperation while working alongside experienced policymakers and communications professionals.

Whether your goal is a career in government, diplomacy, public policy, international organizations, or media, this fellowship offers the knowledge, experience, mentorship, and professional network needed to launch a successful career in international affairs. Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare a strong application package and submit all required materials before the 29 July 2026 deadline.

The post Max Kampelman Fellowships 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid U.S. Helsinki Commission Fellowships for Graduate Students in International Affairs, Human Rights, and Strategic Communications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Young Academy Call for New Members 2027: Apply to Join a Global Network of Outstanding Early-Career Researchers
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Global Young Academy (GYA) has officially opened applications for its 2027 Cohort of New Members, inviting exceptional early- and mid-career researchers from around the world to become part of one of the most prestigious international networks of young scholars. The academy is seeking individuals who demonstrate outstanding research excellence, a commitment to creating positive […]

The post Global Young Academy Call for New Members 2027: Apply to Join a Global Network of Outstanding Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Young Academy (GYA) has officially opened applications for its 2027 Cohort of New Members, inviting exceptional early- and mid-career researchers from around the world to become part of one of the most prestigious international networks of young scholars. The academy is seeking individuals who demonstrate outstanding research excellence, a commitment to creating positive societal impact, and a passion for interdisciplinary collaboration.

Membership in the Global Young Academy is a unique opportunity to collaborate with leading researchers from diverse disciplines and countries while contributing to global discussions on science, education, policy, sustainability, and innovation. The organization brings together outstanding young scholars who are committed not only to advancing research but also to using science and scholarship to address global challenges.

Applications are open to researchers from all disciplines, including the natural sciences, physical sciences, social sciences, arts, and humanities. Professionals conducting research in government institutions, industry, and non-governmental organizations are also encouraged to apply.

About the Global Young Academy

The Global Young Academy is an independent, international, member-led organization established to empower outstanding young researchers to contribute to science and society.

The Academy serves as a platform where talented scholars from across the globe collaborate on issues affecting science, policy, education, innovation, and sustainable development. Members work together on international initiatives that strengthen scientific cooperation, improve public engagement with science, and support evidence-based policymaking.

One of the defining characteristics of the GYA is its diversity. The organization brings together members from different countries, academic disciplines, cultures, and professional backgrounds to promote interdisciplinary collaboration and global knowledge exchange.

Why Become a GYA Member?

Membership in the Global Young Academy offers far more than professional recognition. It provides researchers with opportunities to engage in international leadership, influence science policy, and contribute to initiatives that create meaningful societal impact.

As a member, you will have the opportunity to:

  • Collaborate with outstanding researchers from around the world.
  • Participate in international scientific and policy discussions.
  • Promote science communication and public engagement.
  • Contribute to science diplomacy initiatives.
  • Support education and outreach programmes.
  • Develop leadership skills through committee and project work.
  • Build long-term international research collaborations.
  • Expand your professional network across disciplines and countries.
  • Participate in interdisciplinary global projects.

The GYA emphasizes active participation, making membership especially valuable for researchers who want to shape the future of science beyond their own laboratories or institutions.

Who Should Apply?

The call is open to young, independent scholars who combine research excellence with a demonstrated commitment to making a positive difference in society.

The Academy welcomes applications from individuals working in:

  • Natural Sciences
  • Physical Sciences
  • Social Sciences
  • Arts
  • Humanities
  • Government research institutions
  • Industry research
  • Non-governmental organizations

Applicants from diverse professional backgrounds are encouraged, provided they maintain an active research profile.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet several important eligibility criteria.

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must hold:

  • A PhD or an equivalent doctoral degree.

Researchers who have successfully defended their doctoral thesis but have not yet received the official diploma may also be eligible.

Research Excellence

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • A strong record of research achievements.
  • Evidence of academic excellence.
  • Significant potential for future contributions to their discipline.

Rather than relying on citation metrics or publication counts, the GYA focuses on the overall quality and impact of an applicant’s work.

Societal Impact

Applicants should provide evidence of:

  • Science communication.
  • Public engagement.
  • Policy engagement.
  • Community outreach.
  • Leadership activities.
  • Contributions that extend beyond academic research.

The Academy values researchers who use their expertise to benefit society.

Career Stage

The GYA is designed for researchers in the early to middle stages of their independent careers.

Eligibility is generally based on a combination of age and PhD completion date:

  • Applicants born in or after 1986 with a PhD are eligible.
  • Applicants born before 1986 who obtained their PhD in 2019 or later are also eligible.
  • Those born before 1986 with a PhD obtained before 2019 may still apply if they provide a detailed justification for consideration.

Member Expectations

The Global Young Academy is a highly active, member-led organization.

Successful applicants are expected to:

  • Contribute at least six hours per month to GYA programmes, committees, or projects.
  • Participate in science-for-policy and policy-for-science activities.
  • Support science education and outreach.
  • Engage in international collaborations.
  • Contribute to science diplomacy initiatives.

Members are also encouraged to attend the Annual General Meeting (AGM) each year. Newly selected members should be available to attend the 2027 AGM, scheduled for 21–25 June 2027 in Indonesia.

Application Process

Applications must be completed personally by each candidate and submitted in English through the online application system.

The application includes:

  • Personal information.
  • Several short essay questions.
  • Curriculum Vitae (maximum four pages).
  • One to three letters of support.
  • A signed data protection declaration.

Applicants are encouraged to write authentic responses that reflect their own experiences. The GYA specifically notes that AI-generated responses are unlikely to receive high rankings during the assessment process.

Shortlisted candidates may undergo a second stage of evaluation before final selection.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Membership in the Global Young Academy is widely regarded as one of the most prestigious recognitions for outstanding early-career researchers.

Beyond academic achievement, the Academy values leadership, collaboration, innovation, and societal impact. Members become part of a worldwide community committed to addressing global challenges through research, interdisciplinary cooperation, and evidence-based solutions.

Whether your interests lie in climate change, health, technology, education, public policy, social sciences, engineering, or the humanities, the GYA provides a unique platform to connect with peers from around the world and contribute to meaningful international initiatives.

Researchers who aspire to become global scientific leaders should strongly consider applying for this opportunity.

Application Deadline: 15 September 2026 at 18:00 UTC

Apply Here

For detailed information about eligibility and membership, visit the official call page

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post Global Young Academy Call for New Members 2027: Apply to Join a Global Network of Outstanding Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: U.S. Department of War Cyber Apprenticeship Program 2026: A Paid 12-Month Opportunity to Launch Your Cybersecurity Career
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The United States Department of War has officially opened applications for its 2026 Cyber Apprenticeship Program, a unique 12-month paid apprenticeship designed to prepare aspiring cybersecurity professionals for rewarding careers in one of the world’s fastest-growing industries. Applications opened on 6 July 2026 and will close on 17 July 2026, giving eligible candidates a limited […]

The post U.S. Department of War Cyber Apprenticeship Program 2026: A Paid 12-Month Opportunity to Launch Your Cybersecurity Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United States Department of War has officially opened applications for its 2026 Cyber Apprenticeship Program, a unique 12-month paid apprenticeship designed to prepare aspiring cybersecurity professionals for rewarding careers in one of the world’s fastest-growing industries. Applications opened on 6 July 2026 and will close on 17 July 2026, giving eligible candidates a limited window to apply.

Unlike many cybersecurity training programs that require years of experience or advanced academic qualifications, this apprenticeship focuses on identifying individuals with strong aptitude, motivation, and the willingness to learn. Successful applicants will receive comprehensive training through a combination of online coursework, practical laboratory exercises, and real-world on-the-job experience under the guidance of experienced cybersecurity professionals.

The Department of War has made it clear that this initiative is part of a broader effort to modernize recruitment by prioritizing practical ability and potential over traditional degree requirements. The programme is expected to play a significant role in building the next generation of cybersecurity professionals capable of protecting critical government systems and national digital infrastructure.

About the Cyber Apprenticeship Program

The Cyber Apprenticeship Program is a structured, full-time training initiative that equips participants with the technical knowledge, practical skills, and industry-recognized certifications needed to begin careers in cybersecurity.

Over the course of 12 months, apprentices will receive a balanced learning experience combining:

  • Online technical instruction.
  • Interactive cybersecurity laboratories.
  • Practical workplace experience.
  • Mentorship from senior cybersecurity professionals.
  • Professional certification preparation.

The apprenticeship is specifically designed for individuals who may not yet have professional cybersecurity experience but demonstrate the potential to succeed in technical roles.

Why This Program Matters

Cybersecurity continues to be one of the fastest-growing career fields worldwide. Governments, businesses, healthcare providers, financial institutions, and critical infrastructure organizations all face increasing cyber threats, creating unprecedented demand for skilled cybersecurity professionals.

Recognizing this need, the Department of War is adopting a skills-first hiring model that emphasizes practical ability rather than relying solely on traditional educational pathways.

This initiative provides an alternative route into cybersecurity by allowing talented individuals to learn through structured training while gaining valuable workplace experience.

The program demonstrates a growing recognition that capable cybersecurity professionals can come from diverse educational and professional backgrounds.

What Participants Will Learn

During the apprenticeship, participants will develop practical knowledge across multiple cybersecurity disciplines.

Training includes:

1. Online Learning

Participants complete structured cybersecurity courses covering essential concepts, technologies, and best practices.

2. Hands-On Cybersecurity Labs

Interactive laboratory exercises provide practical experience in identifying, analyzing, and responding to cyber threats.

3. Workplace Training

Apprentices receive real-world experience by working alongside experienced cybersecurity professionals on operational tasks.

4. Professional Mentorship

Each apprentice benefits from guidance and coaching provided by senior cybersecurity mentors.

5. Industry Certifications

Participants earn recognized cybersecurity certifications that strengthen their professional credentials and improve long-term career prospects.

Career Opportunities After Completion

Graduates of the program become eligible for civilian cybersecurity positions within the Department of War.

Potential career paths include:

  • Cyber Defense Analyst
  • Incident Responder
  • Cybersecurity Operations Specialist
  • Network Defense Professional
  • Information Security Analyst
  • Other civilian cybersecurity roles

These positions provide opportunities to continue building expertise while contributing to national cybersecurity efforts.

Two Career Pathways Available

Applicants may qualify under one of two apprenticeship pathways.

Technical Specialist Pathway

This pathway is designed for individuals with strong technical potential and does not require a college degree.

It focuses on practical skills development and provides an accessible entry point into cybersecurity for candidates from diverse educational backgrounds.

Defense Manpower Data Center Agency Pathway

This pathway is intended for applicants who already hold an accredited college degree and are seeking more specialized cybersecurity placements within the Department.

It provides additional opportunities for graduates interested in advanced technical roles.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a United States citizen.
  • Be at least 18 years of age.
  • Be eligible to obtain a Secret Security Clearance.
  • Meet the specific eligibility requirements for the selected pathway.

Importantly, prior professional cybersecurity experience is not required, making this an excellent opportunity for career changers, recent graduates, and individuals seeking to enter the cybersecurity profession.

Why You Should Apply

The Cyber Apprenticeship Program offers numerous advantages for aspiring cybersecurity professionals.

Benefits include:

  • Paid apprenticeship.
  • Twelve months of structured training.
  • Practical hands-on experience.
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals.
  • Industry-recognized certifications.
  • Opportunity to qualify for civilian cybersecurity careers.
  • Alternative pathway that emphasizes skills rather than degrees.
  • Exposure to real-world cybersecurity operations.

This combination of training and professional development makes the programme one of the most attractive entry-level cybersecurity opportunities currently available.

Strong Public Interest

Interest in the apprenticeship has been exceptionally high.

According to the Department of War, a programme preview released in late April generated more than 70,000 inquiries, demonstrating the significant demand for accessible cybersecurity career pathways.

The overwhelming response reflects both the growing popularity of cybersecurity careers and the appeal of skills-based hiring initiatives.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Many entry-level cybersecurity positions require several years of experience or advanced qualifications, creating significant barriers for newcomers.

This apprenticeship challenges that model by investing directly in talent development.

Instead of expecting applicants to already possess extensive experience, the programme equips successful candidates with the knowledge, certifications, and practical experience needed to launch successful cybersecurity careers.

For motivated individuals with strong problem-solving abilities and an interest in technology, this represents an exceptional opportunity to begin a career in one of the world’s most in-demand professions.

Application Deadline: 17 July 2026

Applications opened on 6 July 2026.

Apply here

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post U.S. Department of War Cyber Apprenticeship Program 2026: A Paid 12-Month Opportunity to Launch Your Cybersecurity Career appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 (Fully Funded) | Apply by 26 August 2026
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track 2026–2028, a fully funded two-year fellowship for young public health professionals from African Union Member States. The programme includes three months of in-person training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, followed by 21 months of field-based applied epidemiology training, with travel costs, […]

The post Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 (Fully Funded) | Apply by 26 August 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track 2026–2028, a fully funded two-year fellowship for young public health professionals from African Union Member States. The programme includes three months of in-person training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, followed by 21 months of field-based applied epidemiology training, with travel costs, monthly stipends, health insurance, accommodation-related support, learning materials, hardware, software, and mentorship fully covered. The fellowship begins in October 2026, and applications close on 26 August 2026.

Fellowship at a Glance

  • Host: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC)
  • Programme: African Epidemic Services (AES) – Epidemiology Track
  • Duration: 2 years (24 months)
  • Funding: Fully funded
  • Training Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (first 3 months)
  • Field Placement: 21 months in an African Union Member State
  • Eligible Countries: Citizens of African Union Member States
  • Age Requirement: Under 35 years
  • Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
  • Programme Starts: October 2026

About Africa CDC

The Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention is an autonomous technical Agency established by the African Union (AU) to enhance the capacity, capabilities, and partnerships of Africa’s public health institutions.

Africa CDC is mandated through Article 3 of its governing statute to “support Member States in capacity building in public health through medium and long-term field epidemiologic and laboratory training programmes”. To fulfil this mandate, Africa CDC convened the Africa Health Workforce Compact in 2025, with a focus on investment, training, and retaining of skilled health workforce. The Compact builds upon the Africa CDC Framework for Health Workforce Development 2020–2025 and supports the African Union Agenda 2063 through the development of a sufficient, motivated, and well-trained health workforce capable of delivering essential health services across the continent.

About the Fellowship

Africa CDC has developed a continental African Epidemic Service (AES) Program, a flagship training program aiming to enhance the capacity, capabilities, and partnerships of Africa’s public health institutions. Its primary goal is to build capacity of mid-level professionals to control, prevent, detect, and respond to disease threats and outbreaks by employing science, policy, and data-driven interventions and programs effectively and efficiently whilst learning from best practices across the continent. The African Epidemic Service has three tracks namely, Epidemiology, Laboratory Leadership Fellowship and Public health informatics.

The African Epidemic Services – Epidemiology track is a full time two-year competency based applied epidemiology training program with three months in-person didactic training in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, and 21 months of applied training in a site hosted by an African Union Member State. The program aims to strengthen the disease prevention and response capacity of African Union Member States. Successful applicants will participate in experiential Core Activities of Learning (CALs) that include, but are not limited to, data analysis, surveillance system evaluation, and outbreak investigation. The AES-Epidemiology fellow will have the support of an expert supervisor and or a mentor who will provide support throughout the fellowship programme.

Africa CDC will provide all learning and development materials and meet cover costs associated with the fellowship, including travel-related costs, stipends, hardware, software and health insurance.

Education

Applicants for the Fellowship must:

  1. Be a citizen of an African Union Member State.
  2. Be a youth as defined by the African Union, i.e., an individual under the age of 35.
  3. At the time of application, be employed in an African country with permission from employing government institution to attend the two years training, with guarantee to return to employment post training;
  4. Have a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree from an accredited university in the one health or related fields such as human medicine, nursing veterinary medicine, environmental sciences, public health or related fields or other doctoral-level training;
  5. Demonstrate satisfactory completion of coursework in epidemiology or quantitative research methods; and biostatistics
  6. Have at least three years of recent full-time public health post graduate experience in an African country.
  7. Demonstrate ability to read and prepare technical medical and epidemiological communication materials such as scientific publications, scientific conference presentations etc
  8. Proficiency in at least one of the African Union’s official languages (English, French, Arabic, Portuguese, Spanish, or Kiswahili) is required. Knowledge of an additional AU official language is an asset

Note: Advanced FETP Fellows and Graduates are not eligible to apply.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY OR GET MORE DETAILS 

Requirements

All applications must be submitted using the online application form. Incomplete applications will not be considered. All applicants must provide the following information:

  1. A national passport from an African Union Member State.
  2. An official letter of support with permission from a government institution to attend the two-year training programme with the right to return post training.
  3. Letters of recommendation from two reputable professional referees.
  4. An official copy of degree certificate and transcripts of your bachelor’s or master’s degree.
  5. Brief curriculum vitae using the AU template.
  6. Motivation statement (maximum 500 words), outlining why this program would be a good fit, and further outlining how this training will impact your country.
  7. Optional: Evidence of additional relevant qualifications.

Important Information

Completed applications with all supporting documents should be uploaded to the online portal. Incomplete applications will not be considered. The fellowship program will prioritize inclusivity, gender equity, and representation from across the continent.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
  • Programme Start Date: October 2026

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the online application form and upload all required supporting documents before the deadline. Incomplete applications will not be considered.

The fellowship promotes gender equality, inclusivity, and broad representation across African Union Member States.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY OR GET MORE DETAILS 

The Africa CDC African Epidemic Services (AES) Epidemiology Fellowship 2026–2028 is an outstanding opportunity for young public health professionals to receive world-class epidemiology training while helping strengthen disease surveillance and outbreak response systems across Africa.

Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
